zh-CN.title: Spec-Driven Development 深度指南 — Part 2: GitHub Spec Kit 实战指南
zh-CN.description: 深入了解 GitHub Spec Kit v0.12.4 的 10 个内置命令、核心 SDD 生命周期与 implement/converge 收敛循环，减少 AI 编码中的规范漂移。
zh-CN.content:
更新说明（2026-07-03）：本文已按 GitHub Spec Kit v0.12.4 的官方命令表与 command templates 重新核验。当前版本包含 10 个内置命令，其中 /speckit.converge 是相较初稿新增的重要收敛阶段。

GitHub Spec Kit 是一套由 GitHub 推出的开源工具包，通过 10 个内置命令（如 /speckit.specify、/speckit.plan 和 /speckit.converge）组织规范驱动开发（SDD）。它以持久化 artifacts 约束大型语言模型（LLM）的工作过程，降低代码实现与业务意图之间发生“规范漂移（Spec Drift）”的概率。

Series Navigation:

  • Part 1: 软件设计的演进与 SDD 的本质
  • Part 2 (This Post): GitHub Spec Kit 实战指南
  • Part 3: 意图层基础设施与 SDD 的未来
  • Part 4: 使用 GitHub Issues 构建简单的 SDD 工作流


Illustration: GitHub Spec Kit — 将意图规范封装为高度体系化的命令行基础设施

1. 什么是 Spec Kit？

GitHub Spec Kit 是 GitHub 于 2025 年 8 月开源的规范驱动开发（Spec-Driven Development, SDD）工具包。它并非替代 AI 编码助手，而是通过 CLI、结构化模板与 agent commands 管理开发 artifacts，并适配多种 AI coding agents。

核心理念：变迁真理唯一来源

在传统工作流里，一旦业务开发完成，文档立刻生锈（即规范漂移，Spec Drift）。团队倾向于把代码作为绝对裁定的事实孤岛（Source of Truth）。

但在 Spec Kit 赋能下，规范直接等同于代码构建配方。当你改变主意，只需修改规范文档，AI 直接依图纸重新编译修改产物代码，而不是让开发者痛苦修改屎山。这种理念与利用强大的 Model Context Protocol (MCP) 打破工具孤岛的思维拥有异曲同工之妙。

主要适合场景：

  • 零起步的 Greenfield 绿地项目
  • 对复杂意图具有高度定制化约束要求的现代化迭代工程
  • 具有刚性多人协同并避免跨终端系统偏差的大型复杂生态群
术语 (Term) 定义
Spec Kit GitHub 开源的 SDD 工具包，包含一套 Slash Commands 和标准模板。
Spec Drift 规范漂移。代码实现与业务文档随时间产生的不一致现象。
Artifacts 工件。指生命周期中生成的 spec.md、plan.md、tasks.md 等事实依据。

2. GitHub Spec Kit v0.12.4：7 个 Core + 3 个 Optional

官方 README 将 10 个内置命令分为 Core Commands 与 Optional Commands。这里的 Optional 表示它们不是最短 happy path 的必经步骤，并不代表价值较低。

Core Commands

Command 作用 主要产物或效果
/speckit.constitution 创建或更新项目治理原则，并同步检查相关模板 .specify/memory/constitution.md
/speckit.specify 从自然语言描述创建或更新 feature specification spec.md 与 requirements checklist
/speckit.plan 根据技术栈和架构决策生成实现计划 plan.md、research.md 等设计 artifacts
/speckit.tasks 生成按 user story 与依赖排序的可执行任务 tasks.md
/speckit.taskstoissues 将 task 转换为去重、依赖有序的 GitHub Issues GitHub Issues
/speckit.implement 按 tasks.md 执行实现并回写完成状态 代码、测试与 [X] task state
/speckit.converge 对照 spec/plan/tasks 评估当前代码，将剩余工作追加为新任务 append-only Convergence phase

Optional Commands

  • /speckit.clarify：识别 specification 中定义不足的区域，逐次提出最多 5 个高影响问题，并把答案写回 spec。官方建议在 /speckit.plan 前完成。
  • /speckit.analyze：在 /speckit.tasks 之后、/speckit.implement 之前，对 spec、plan 与 tasks 做只读的一致性和覆盖率分析。
  • /speckit.checklist：围绕指定主题生成“requirements 的单元测试”，检查需求是否完整、清晰且一致；它检查的是需求文本，不是运行中的代码行为。

当前推荐生命周期

最短主线仍然是 constitution → specify → plan → tasks → implement。面向复杂或长期演进的 feature，更完整的生命周期会加入 clarify、analyze 与 converge：

sequenceDiagram
    autonumber
    participant U as 👤 Human (User)
    participant A as 🧠 AI Agent (Spec-Kit)
    participant F as 📄 Artifacts (文档/产物)
    participant G as GitHub

    Note over U, F: [阶段 1: Genesis - 立宪]
    U->>A: /speckit.constitution (治理原则)
    A->>F: 写入规则与技术栈约束

    Note over U, F: [阶段 2: Definition - 意图定义]
    U->>A: /speckit.specify (业务规格)
    A->>F: 生成 Spec.md
    
    Note over U, F: [阶段 3: Clarification - 推荐在 Plan 前完成]
    U->>A: /speckit.clarify (人机对齐)
    A->>F: 逐次提问并回填 spec.md

    Note over U, F: [阶段 4: Design - 技术规划]
    U->>A: /speckit.plan (技术规划)
    A->>F: 生成 plan.md 与设计 artifacts

    Note over U, F: [阶段 5: Engineering - 任务拆解]
    U->>A: /speckit.tasks (任务解耦)
    A->>F: 生成 tasks.md

    Note over U, F: [阶段 6: Audit & Sync - 审计与同步]
    U->>A: /speckit.analyze (三方全产物审计)
    A-->>U: 只读一致性与覆盖率报告
    
    opt 协同扩展
        U->>A: /speckit.taskstoissues
        A->>G: 同步本地 Tasks 至 GitHub Issues
    end

    Note over U, F: [阶段 7: Execution - 意图落地]
    U->>A: /speckit.implement (自动化编码)
    A->>F: 执行 tasks 并回写 [X]

    Note over U, F: [阶段 8: Convergence - 实现后收敛]
    U->>A: /speckit.converge
    A->>F: 对照意图评估当前代码
    alt 仍有未完成工作
        A->>F: 追加 Convergence tasks
        U->>A: 再次 /speckit.implement
    else 已完全收敛
        A-->>U: Converged
    end

3. 四种不同的质量反馈机制

旧版本文章容易把 clarify、checklist、analyze 与 converge 都概括成“质量门禁”，但它们的写入边界与使用时机不同：

Command 主要问题 是否修改 artifact 建议时机
/speckit.clarify specification 还有哪些高影响歧义？ 是，写回 spec.md specify 之后、plan 之前
/speckit.checklist requirements 本身是否完整、清晰、一致？ 是，创建或追加 checklist 需要专项需求审查时
/speckit.analyze spec、plan、tasks 之间是否冲突或覆盖不足？ 否，只读分析 tasks 之后、implement 之前
/speckit.converge 当前代码距离既定 artifacts 还差什么？ 是，只向 tasks.md 追加任务 implement 之后

/speckit.implement 启动时会统计 checklists/ 中的完成状态。若发现未完成项，它会停止并询问用户是否仍要继续；用户可以明确选择继续。因此，这是一道显式决策门，而不是底层脚本无条件锁死代码生成，也不会因为没有运行 clarify 自动拒绝实现。

/speckit.converge 则补上了旧流程中缺失的实现后反馈环。它不改写 spec 或 plan，也不直接修代码；唯一允许的写入是把 missing、partial、contradicts 或 unrequested 等发现追加为新的 Convergence tasks，再交回 /speckit.implement 完成。

4. 最佳实践：专家建议技巧

A. 验收标准必须强制“可度量化与可触发”

  • ❌ 错误做法：“系统保证流畅稳定，不要出错。”
  • ✅ 正确做法：“AC2：API QPS 阈值需应对大于 200，且在超时 1.5s 后触发主动缓存切流。”

B. Bug 出现后的处置逻辑分流

如果代码实施中跑出了错误。该修改代码还是修改文档？

  1. 执行层失败：如果代码逻辑符合 Spec 但因环境或语法微瑕出错，让 Agent 直接提供 Patch。
  2. 逻辑遗漏：不要只修源码。先把遗漏规则写回 spec.md 或 plan.md，再重新运行受影响的下游阶段，让 artifacts 与实现重新对齐并降低 Spec Drift 风险。

References

  • GitHub Spec Kit v0.12.4 README：Available Slash Commands
  • /speckit.converge command template
  • /speckit.implement command template

What’s Next：SDD 的未来演进

GitHub Spec Kit 通过 10 个内置命令，把需求定义、技术规划、任务执行与实现后收敛连接成可追踪的 artifact lifecycle。这种转变为 AI 代理 (AI Agents) 的深度协作提供了比一次性 prompt 更稳定的意图基础设施。

而在下一篇文章 Part 3: 意图层基础设施与 SDD 的未来 中，我们将探讨随着 LLM 自管记忆能力的迅猛攀升，SDD 将如何演变为完全自主迭代的新型“自治控制面”。

Series Navigation:

  • ← Previous: Part 1: 软件设计的演进与 SDD 的本质
  • → Next: Part 3: 意图层基础设施与 SDD 的未来

en.title: Spec-Driven Development Deep Dive — Part 2: GitHub Spec Kit Practical Guide
en.description: Gain a deeper understanding of the 10 built-in commands, core SDD lifecycle, and implement/converge convergence loops in GitHub Spec Kit v0.12.4 to reduce specification drift in AI coding.
en.content:
Update Notes (2026-07-03)This article is compliant with the GitHub Spec Kit.v0.12.4The official command list and command templates have been re-verified. The current version includes 10 built-in commands, of which.../speckit.convergeThis is an important convergence stage that has been added compared to the initial draft.

GitHub Spec KitIt is an open-source toolkit released by GitHub, with 10 built-in commands (such as...)/speckit.specify、/speckit.plan and /speckit.convergeOrganizational Specification-Driven Development (SDD) constrains the working process of Large Language Models (LLMs) with persistent artifacts, reducing the probability of "Spec Drift" between code implementation and business intent.

Series Navigation:

  • Part 1: The Evolution of Software Design and the Essence of SDD
  • Part 2 (This Post): GitHub Spec Kit Practical Guide
  • Part 3: Intent Layer Infrastructure and the Future of SDD
  • Part 4: Building a Simple SDD Workflow Using GitHub Issues


Illustration: GitHub Spec Kit — Encapsulating intent specifications into a highly systematic command-line infrastructure

1. What is a Spec Kit?

GitHub Spec Kit is a specification-driven development (SDD) toolkit released by GitHub in August 2025. It is not a replacement for AI coding assistants, but rather manages and develops artifacts through a CLI, structured templates, and agent commands, and is compatible with various AI coding agents.

Core concept: The sole source of truth in change

In traditional workflows, once business development is complete, documentation immediately becomes rusty (i.e., specification drift). Teams tend to treat code as an isolated source of truth.

However, with the help of the Spec Kit,Specifications are directly equivalent to code build recipesWhen you change your mind, simply modify the specification document, and AI will directly recompile and modify the output code according to the drawings, instead of making developers painfully modify mountains of code. This concept leverages powerful AI capabilities.Model Context Protocol (MCP)The idea of ​​breaking down tool silos has a similar underlying principle.

Main suitable scenarios:

  • Greenfield project starting from scratch
  • Modern iterative engineering with highly customized constraints for complex intentions
  • Large and complex ecosystems with rigid multi-user collaboration and avoidance of deviations across terminal systems
术语 (Term) 定义
Spec Kit GitHub 开源的 SDD 工具包，包含一套 Slash Commands 和标准模板。
Spec Drift 规范漂移。代码实现与业务文档随时间产生的不一致现象。
Artifacts 工件。指生命周期中生成的 spec.md、plan.md、tasks.md 等事实依据。

2. GitHub Spec Kit v0.12.4: 7 Cores + 3 Optionals

The official README divides the 10 built-in commands into...Core Commands and Optional CommandsThe "Optional" here means that these are not necessary steps in the shortest happy path, and does not imply lower value.

Core Commands

Command 作用 主要产物或效果
/speckit.constitution 创建或更新项目治理原则，并同步检查相关模板 .specify/memory/constitution.md
/speckit.specify 从自然语言描述创建或更新 feature specification spec.md 与 requirements checklist
/speckit.plan 根据技术栈和架构决策生成实现计划 plan.md、research.md 等设计 artifacts
/speckit.tasks 生成按 user story 与依赖排序的可执行任务 tasks.md
/speckit.taskstoissues 将 task 转换为去重、依赖有序的 GitHub Issues GitHub Issues
/speckit.implement 按 tasks.md 执行实现并回写完成状态 代码、测试与 [X] task state
/speckit.converge 对照 spec/plan/tasks 评估当前代码，将剩余工作追加为新任务 append-only Convergence phase

Optional Commands

  • /speckit.clarifyIdentify areas in the specification that are poorly defined, raise up to 5 high-impact questions, and write the answers back to the specification. The official recommendation is to.../speckit.planCompleted previously.
  • /speckit.analyze:exist /speckit.tasks after,/speckit.implementPreviously, read-only consistency and coverage analyses were performed on the spec, plan, and tasks.
  • /speckit.checklistGenerates unit tests for the specified requirements, checking whether the requirements are complete, clear and consistent; it checks the requirement text, not the behavior of the code during runtime.

Current Recommended Lifecycle

The shortest lifecycle remains constitution → specify → plan → tasks → implement. For complex or long-term evolving features, a more complete lifecycle will include clarify, analyze, and converge.

sequenceDiagram
    autonumber
    participant U as 👤 Human (User)
    participant A as 🧠 AI Agent (Spec-Kit)
    participant F as 📄 Artifacts (文档/产物)
    participant G as GitHub

    Note over U, F: [阶段 1: Genesis - 立宪]
    U->>A: /speckit.constitution (治理原则)
    A->>F: 写入规则与技术栈约束

    Note over U, F: [阶段 2: Definition - 意图定义]
    U->>A: /speckit.specify (业务规格)
    A->>F: 生成 Spec.md
    
    Note over U, F: [阶段 3: Clarification - 推荐在 Plan 前完成]
    U->>A: /speckit.clarify (人机对齐)
    A->>F: 逐次提问并回填 spec.md

    Note over U, F: [阶段 4: Design - 技术规划]
    U->>A: /speckit.plan (技术规划)
    A->>F: 生成 plan.md 与设计 artifacts

    Note over U, F: [阶段 5: Engineering - 任务拆解]
    U->>A: /speckit.tasks (任务解耦)
    A->>F: 生成 tasks.md

    Note over U, F: [阶段 6: Audit & Sync - 审计与同步]
    U->>A: /speckit.analyze (三方全产物审计)
    A-->>U: 只读一致性与覆盖率报告
    
    opt 协同扩展
        U->>A: /speckit.taskstoissues
        A->>G: 同步本地 Tasks 至 GitHub Issues
    end

    Note over U, F: [阶段 7: Execution - 意图落地]
    U->>A: /speckit.implement (自动化编码)
    A->>F: 执行 tasks 并回写 [X]

    Note over U, F: [阶段 8: Convergence - 实现后收敛]
    U->>A: /speckit.converge
    A->>F: 对照意图评估当前代码
    alt 仍有未完成工作
        A->>F: 追加 Convergence tasks
        U->>A: 再次 /speckit.implement
    else 已完全收敛
        A-->>U: Converged
    end

3. Four different quality feedback mechanisms

Older versions of the article tended to categorize clarify, checklist, analyze, and converge as "quality gates," but their write boundaries and usage scenarios differ:

Command 主要问题 是否修改 artifact 建议时机
/speckit.clarify specification 还有哪些高影响歧义？ 是，写回 spec.md specify 之后、plan 之前
/speckit.checklist requirements 本身是否完整、清晰、一致？ 是，创建或追加 checklist 需要专项需求审查时
/speckit.analyze spec、plan、tasks 之间是否冲突或覆盖不足？ 否，只读分析 tasks 之后、implement 之前
/speckit.converge 当前代码距离既定 artifacts 还差什么？ 是，只向 tasks.md 追加任务 implement 之后

/speckit.implementStatistics will be collected during startup.checklists/The system displays the completion status. If an incomplete item is found, it stops and asks the user if they still want to continue; the user can explicitly choose to continue. Therefore, this is an explicit decision gate, rather than an unconditional lockout of code generation by the underlying script, and it does not automatically reject implementation due to the absence of `clarify`.

/speckit.convergeThis fills the gap in the old process by eliminating the post-implementation feedback loop. It doesn't rewrite the spec or plan, nor does it directly modify the code; the only allowed write is to...missing、partial、contradicts or unrequestedOnce it's discovered that it's been added as a new Convergence task, then submit it back./speckit.implement Finish.

4. Best Practices: Expert Advice Techniques

A. Acceptance criteria must be mandatory and "measurable and triggerable".

  • ❌ Incorrect practice"The system must be smooth and stable, and free from errors."
  • ✅ Correct approach"AC2: The API QPS threshold needs to be greater than 200, and active caching and flow switching should be triggered after a timeout of 1.5 seconds."

B. Logic decentralization for handling bugs after they occur

If an error occurs during code implementation, should we modify the code or the documentation?

  1. Execution layer failureIf the code logic conforms to the Spec but is incorrect due to minor environmental or syntactic flaws, let the Agent provide a patch directly.
  2. Logical omissionDon't just fix the source code. First, rewrite the missing rules.spec.md or plan.mdThen, rerun the affected downstream stages to realign artifacts with the implementation and reduce the risk of Spec Drift.

References

  • GitHub Spec Kit v0.12.4 README：Available Slash Commands
  • /speckit.converge command template
  • /speckit.implement command template

What’s Next: The Future Evolution of SDD

GitHub Spec KitThrough 10 built-in commands, the process of requirement definition, technology planning, task execution, and post-implementation convergence is connected into a traceable artifact lifecycle. This transformation...AI AgentsDeep collaboration provides a more stable intent infrastructure than one-off prompts.

In the next articlePart 3: Intent Layer Infrastructure and the Future of SDDIn this article, we will explore how SDD will evolve into a new type of "autonomous control surface" that is fully autonomous and iterative, as LLM's self-managed memory capacity rapidly increases.

Series Navigation:

  • ← Previous: Part 1: The Evolution of Software Design and the Essence of SDD
  • → Next: Part 3: Intent Layer Infrastructure and the Future of SDD
